channel.title: All Posts - zqw's notes
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

item.title: Dive into K8s 02: Cilium Replaces Flannel — Goodbye VXLAN Tax, Hello eBPF Native Networking
item.author: Paul
item.description: Last post, we hand-rolled Flannel in Kind cluster and witnessed the 50-byte per-packet VXLAN encapsulation overhead via tcpdump. This time, we replace Flannel with Cilium, leveraging eBPF to completely eliminate this “network tax”, and use Hubble for traffic visualization.

item.title: Dive into K8s: Hand-Rolling CNI with Kind, From CrashLoop to VXLAN Packet Capture
item.author: Paul
item.description: This weekend, I built a “networkless” cluster from scratch on Manjaro using kind, manually debugged missing kernel modules and CNI plugins, and finally witnessed the VXLAN encapsulation process with tcpdump. This post documents the entire journey.

item.title: Temporal vs K8s Controller: Declarative vs Imperative Control Plane Paradigms
item.author: Paul
item.description: K8s Controllers and Temporal are both “control plane” technologies, but they represent fundamentally different design philosophies. This post deep-dives into their core differences and explores how to combine them in AI Infra platforms.

item.title: Temporal in Practice: From Saga Pattern to Self-Healing Distributed Transactions
item.author: Paul
item.description: While building a trading system backend, I used Temporal to solve the classic distributed transaction consistency problem. This post breaks down Temporal’s core mechanisms and explores its applications in AI task scheduling.

item.title: Go Lock Performance Traps: From sync.Mutex to Spinlock Tuning
item.author: Paul
item.description: A seemingly simple concurrent Map access, performance crashes under high concurrency. pprof shows 90% of time spent in sync.Mutex.Lock. This post documents the investigation and performance comparison of different lock strategies.

item.title: Custom Linux Distro: From Yocto Build to 60% Boot Time Optimization
item.author: Paul
item.description: A key user experience metric for NAS products: how long from pressing power to accessing shared folders? Our target was 30 seconds, but the original took 75 seconds. This post documents the complete journey from 75s to 30s.

item.title: Go Daemon Development: Graceful SIGTERM Handling and Config Hot-Reload
item.author: Paul
item.description: For a NAS product, I needed to develop a 24/7 system daemon for disk health monitoring and scheduled task management. This post covers the key technical aspects: signal handling, graceful shutdown, and config hot-reload.

item.title: NAS Disk Health Monitoring: From smartctl to Custom Agent
item.author: Paul
item.description: A NAS’s most important job is protecting user data. Disk failures often have warning signs, and SMART technology can detect problems early. This post documents how to build a disk health monitoring agent.

item.title: Production-Grade String Parsing: From atoi to State Machine
item.author: Paul
item.description: LeetCode’s atoi is a classic interview problem, but production-grade implementations are far more complex. This post starts from the interview version and gradually analyzes Go and Rust standard library implementations to understand “production-grade” code design.

item.title: Go Generics in Practice: Pitfalls and Best Practices
item.author: Paul
item.description: Go 1.18 finally brought generics. In our NAS project, we refactored several common modules using generics. This post documents real-world benefits, pitfalls, and best practices we learned.
